current_activity: Plan the backend discovery lane as one read-only worker task that narrows MVP backend scope, backend-owned contract questions, and open persistence decisions without producing durable lane outputs or cross-lane handoffs.
Fast-path discovery plan for Simple backend API and persistence layer for storing todo items: let the backend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Plan the frontend discovery lane as one read-only task that bounds the MVP React todo experience, frontend-owned questions, and future UI-facing integration needs without creating shared contracts or outbound handoffs.
Fast-path discovery plan for React web frontend for creating, viewing, completing, editing, and deleting todo items: let the frontend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Plan the middleware discovery lane as one read-only worker task that reconciles integration boundaries, unresolved contract questions, and downstream dependency order from the provided planning inputs without creating durable lane artifacts.
Fast-path discovery plan for Basic application integration and delivery workflow connecting frontend and backend: let the middleware manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Backend discovery scope is reconciled to MVP todo CRUD, persistence, and server-side reliability only. Remaining backend discovery unknowns are limited to stack choice, persistence approach, validation depth, and the backend-owned API contract questions that should be carried into design rather than materialized now.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Middleware discovery scope was reconciled from the injected planning inputs. The lane boundary is limited to application-level integration responsibilities for a simple React frontend and backend todo API, with frontend and backend implementation remaining external dependencies. The missing canonical integration contract was treated as an explicit unresolved design-phase input rather than assumed.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Frontend discovery scope is bounded to the MVP React todo UI: create, view, edit, complete, uncomplete, and delete flows only. Consumer-side constraints, unknowns, and dependency questions were captured from the injected planning inputs without redefining shared backend or integration contracts.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.
Single-task frontend design lane that reconciles discovery inputs into one frontend-only design contract assertion for the React todo MVP.
Fast-path design plan for React web frontend for creating, viewing, completing, editing, and deleting todo items: let the frontend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.
Single-task backend design reconciliation plan for the todo MVP backend lane, covering backend-owned API shape, persistence approach, and validation criteria with no outbound handoffs.
Fast-path design plan for Simple backend API and persistence layer for storing todo items: let the backend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Design planning for the middleware lane stays intentionally minimal in this phase: one read-only reconciliation task confirms the integration boundary, preserves middleware ownership of the shared integration contract, and records that downstream build work must wait for frontend and backend design outputs. No standalone final lane artifacts or outbound handoffs are allowed in this phase.
Fast-path design plan for Basic application integration and delivery workflow connecting frontend and backend: let the middleware manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Frontend design contract is fully specified at the assertion level for the MVP React todo lane: a simple React client supports create, view, edit, complete, uncomplete, and delete flows; keeps UI responsibilities on the frontend for form state, optimistic/pending/error presentation, and list/item rendering; treats persistence and data authority as backend-owned; and constrains consumer-side API expectations to fetch/create/update/delete todo operations sufficient to persist edits and completion state across reloads. Validation criteria remain aligned with discovery scope and planning success criteria without redefining shared backend or middleware contracts.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Backend design is reconciled to a single MVP position consistent with the approved discovery scope: a simple backend-owned REST JSON CRUD surface for todos (`GET /todos`, `POST /todos`, `PATCH /todos/:id`, `DELETE /todos/:id`), a server-managed todo shape (`id`, `title`, `completed`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`), durable SQLite persistence behind a repository/service boundary, and intentionally shallow server-side validation limited to required trimmed title content, bounded payload shape, boolean completion state, immutable server-managed fields, and standard `400`/`404` error handling. This keeps the architecture simple, preserves backend contract authority, and does not redefine frontend or middleware responsibilities.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.
